interpretation: vLLM dense Marlin is not materially faster than Ferrum default hot kernel for gate_up; this falsifies the dense-vLLM-kernel-swap hypothesis for the main gate_up hot path.

Down projection is close to Ferrum hot-kernel timing but much faster than Ferrum weight-cycle timing, matching the product profile down per-layer timing and pointing at weight residency/cache behavior rather than pure kernel selection.

Remaining c16 gap should be pursued in product integration: weight residency/cache cycling, launch/host scheduling, and end-to-end decode overhead, not another full gate or blind vLLM paged-attn flip.
